page: Microsoft Azure ND96amsr_A100_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the NDAMSv4_A100 family. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD processors and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 1800.0 GB of system memory, providing 18.75 GB of RAM per core, which positions it as a memory-intensive instance.

This server integrates eight GPUs as indicated by its family classification, alongside 10650 GB of local storage. It supports Premium Storage and is RDMA capable, enabling high-throughput, low-latency networking and inter-node communication. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance.

Given the high density of dedicated AMD vCPUs, substantial RAM capacity, local storage, and multiple GPUs, this instance represents a high-resource tier with corresponding qualitative infrastructure costs. It is designed for workloads requiring massive parallel processing and memory capacity, such as large-scale machine learning training, deep learning, and complex scientific simulations.
description: Microsoft Azure ND96amsr_A100_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server in the NDAMSv4_A100 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated AMD vCPUs and 1800.0 GB of system memory, yielding 18.75 GB of RAM per core. Designed for memory-intensive and accelerated workloads, the instance includes eight GPUs and 10650 GB of local storage. It is Premium Storage capable and supports RDMA for high-throughput networking. With no complimentary public IPv4 addresses, this server represents a high-density hardware profile suited for resource-intensive deployments. The combination of massive memory, dedicated processing cores, local storage, and multiple GPUs makes this instance appropriate for large-scale parallel computing, machine learning model training, and data-intensive scientific simulations.

tagline: An accelerated, memory-intensive instance featuring ninety-six dedicated cores, eight GPUs, and over ten terabytes of local storage.
